title: ServeMux Patterns, Precedence, Path Values, and Route Safety · Runtime Contract: Route Registration Gate
summary: A Go 1.26 service mixes legacy prefix routes with method and wildcard patterns, conflicts at startup, misroutes escaped slashes, and assumes slash redirects still use a permanent status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo route registration gate increment in validated ServeMux route and precedence table. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compile route registration, recover only to report startup conflicts, enumerate method-path cases, and test near-miss patterns.
Account for GET matching HEAD, method-specific 405 Allow responses, OPTIONS behavior, host routes, and Go 1.26 temporary slash redirects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Route Registration Gate · Theory Route Registration Gate
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9382 in a public status API serving assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose a specific method-and-path route and preserve one registration owner; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Route Registration Gate contract for the supplied data.
